explanation: The contract calls token.transfer or token.transferFrom without checking the boolean return value. Many ERC20 tokens signal failure by returning false instead of reverting, so an ignored return value lets the surrounding logic continue as if the transfer succeeded. Worse, some widely used tokens (USDT among them) do not return a bool at all, so a plain interface call to them can behave inconsistently.
impact: The contract can credit deposits or mark payments as complete when no tokens actually moved, letting an attacker mint phantom balances or withdraw funds they never deposited.
